sections.name: Ruzadoya's Chosen

link.alt: Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root pg. 85
link.name: Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root pg. 85

sections.text: Those who survive the blessing become Ruzadoya's favored.

link.alt: Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root pg. 85
link.name: Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root pg. 85

changes.text: - Increase its level, AC, attack modifiers, DCs, saving throws, and skill modifiers by 1.
- It gains the wood trait, as well as weakness to fire and mental based on its level.
- It gains a splinter ranged Strike that deals piercing damage, is in the brawling group, and has a range increment of 30 feet.

abilities.name: Explosive End
abilities.text: When the woodblessed dies, the planar portal inside of them collapses, causing them to explode in a 20-foot burst of vines that deal 1d6 bludgeoning damage per level to all creatures with a basic Reflex save against the woodblessed's spell DC. The woodblessed's body is completely consumed, though their root network and gear remain behind.

traits.name: Primal
traits.text:
This magic comes from the primal tradition, connecting to the natural world and instinct. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to primal magic.

traits.name: Wood
traits.text:
Effects with the wood trait conjure or manipulate wood. Those that manipulate wood have no effect in an area without wood. Creatures with this trait consist primarily of wood or have a connection to magical wood.  
  
These planes consist of trees and other flora latticed in organic patterns. Wood planes are often perfectly constructed to match their desired purpose or environment but aren’t usually outright hostile to visitors.

abilities.name: Luminant Aura
abilities.text: The volatile planar energy coursing through a woodblessed's veins fills the area with dim light.

traits.name: Aura
traits.text: An aura is an emanation that continually ebbs out from you, affecting creatures within a certain radius. Aura can also refer to the magical signature of an item.

traits.name: Light
traits.text: Light effects overcome non-magical darkness in the area and can counteract magical darkness. You must usually target darkness magic with your light magic directly to counteract the darkness, but some light spells automatically attempt to counteract darkness.

abilities.name: Primal Innate Spells
abilities.text: A woodblessed can cast *timber* as an innate primal cantrip.

abilities.effect: Distracted by the pain, the woodblessed's concentration slips and splinters erupt from their body as they temporarily lose control of the planar energy within them. Both the triggering creature and the woodblessed take 1d6 piercing damage + 1d6 per 2 levels the woodblessed has, with a basic Reflex save against the woodblessed's spell DC.
abilities.name: Reactive Flare
abilities.trigger: The woodblessed is damaged by a creature within their reach

abilities.name: Tap Planar Gate
abilities.text: The woodblessed empowers themself or their next attack.

traits.name: Concentrate
traits.text: An action with this trait requires a degree of mental concentration and discipline.

traits.name: Primal
traits.text:
This magic comes from the primal tradition, connecting to the natural world and instinct. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to primal magic.

subtypes.name: Woodblessed Abilities
subtypes.text: All woodblessed gain the following abilities.

family.name: Woodblessed

link.alt: Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root pg. 85
link.name: Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root pg. 85

family.text: Woodblessed are those granted uncontrollable energy drawn from the Plane of Wood—a blessing that brings pain and eventual death.

traits.name: Uncommon
traits.text: Something of uncommon rarity requires special training or comes from a particular culture or part of the world. Some character choices give access to uncommon options, and the GM can choose to allow access for anyone. Less is known about uncommon creatures than common creatures. They typically can't be summoned. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to these creature is increased by 2.

traits.name: Human
traits.text: A creature with this trait is a member of the human ancestry. Humans are a diverse array of people known for their adaptability. An ability with this trait can be used or selected only by humans.

traits.name: Humanoid
traits.text: Humanoid creatures reason and act much like humans. They typically stand upright and have two arms and two legs.

traits.name: Wood
traits.text:
Effects with the wood trait conjure or manipulate wood. Those that manipulate wood have no effect in an area without wood. Creatures with this trait consist primarily of wood or have a connection to magical wood.  
  
These planes consist of trees and other flora latticed in organic patterns. Wood planes are often perfectly constructed to match their desired purpose or environment but aren’t usually outright hostile to visitors.

traits.name: Light

traits.name: Two-Hand
traits.text: This weapon can be wielded with two hands to change its weapon damage die to the indicated value. This change applies to all the weapon’s damage dice.

traits.name: Range
traits.text: These attacks will either list a finite range or a range increment, which follows the normal rules for range increments.
